notes: Persistent checklist for the discovery agent. Each weekly run processes as many 'pending' items as fits in its time window (in the order defined by priority_order) and writes the updated queue back. When every item is marked 'completed', the pass_number increments and all items are reset to 'pending' for the next verification cycle. Do NOT delete this file between runs.

status_vocabulary.pending: not yet checked in this pass
status_vocabulary.in_progress: partially checked (e.g., fetched judge page but not PDFs)
status_vocabulary.blocked: couldn't complete (URL broken, PDF unreadable) — retry next pass

result_vocabulary.rule_found: an AI rule exists; a rule entry was created or updated (result.rule_id points to it)
result_vocabulary.court_covered_by_court_wide_rule: no individual-judge rule; the court has a court-wide rule that covers this judge (result.rule_id points to the court-wide entry)
result_vocabulary.no_ai_policy: no AI rule found; no entry created in rules.json
result_vocabulary.no_longer_on_bench: judge has retired/elevated/left; any prior entry marked superseded_by=WITHDRAWN
result_vocabulary.url_unreachable: court or judge page did not load; blocked, retry next pass

error_prone_seeds.note: Standing order requires disclosure & certification. Prior run linked to all-judges listing and miscategorized as 'permitted' — must use judge-specific page and read the actual standing order PDF.
Court-wide AI local rule. Prior run misattributed to Judge Starr individually. Correct source is CIVRULES.pdf on the court's rules-and-orders page.
Requires verification 'through traditional methods' — category is disclosure_with_traditional_verification, NOT prohibited_except_assisted_research.
No longer listed on Court of International Trade judges page. Verify status each pass; likely WITHDRAWN.

Standing order cross-references Illinois Supreme Court AI policy, which says disclosure of AI use 'should not be required.' Correct category is permitted_with_caution, not disclosure_required. Follow cross-references.
Now a senior judge, not an active judge. Prior run used active-judges URL and got a 404. Use senior-judges listing.
Verify current status on N.D. Ill. judges page (active and senior). Prior run used an incorrect URL.
She is a C.D. Cal. judge — a prior run incorrectly assigned her to N.D. Cal. Use her C.D. Cal. judge-specific page.
Proposed circuit rule on AI. rule_type should be 'proposed_local_rule'. Prior run used generic 5th Cir. URL — find the specific proposed-rule page or PDF.
AI guidelines live directly on his judge-specific page in an 'Artificial Intelligence' dropdown section. source_url should be the judge page (no PDF needed).

items.note: PDF inaccessible; conflicting information about order content. Needs retry with better PDF access or direct court confirmation.
Verified court-wide Local Civil Rule 7.2(f). Entry updated with latest info.
Verified correctly categorized as disclosure_with_traditional_verification.
Vaden resigned from CIT on July 7, 2025, to become Deputy Secretary of Agriculture. Entry already marked as WITHDRAWN.
Verified correctly categorized as permitted_with_caution. Standing order adopts Illinois Supreme Court AI policy (permits AI, disclosure should not be required).
Verified as senior judge. Standing order PDF confirmed at correct URL for senior judges.
Cole IS active as magistrate judge, but no AI standing order found on his judge page. Prior entry appears to be spurious seed; marked as WITHDRAWN.
Verified correctly as C.D. Cal. judge with disclosure_required standing order from October 2025.
Proposed rule was NOT adopted per June 2024 decision. Entry correctly marked as WITHDRAWN with proper source_url to court decision PDF.
Verified permitted_with_caution standing order at judge-specific page with AI dropdown section.
Verified prohibited_except_assisted_research. Standing order prohibits AI except for legal/internet search engines.
Verified disclosure_required. General Pretrial and Trial Procedures require mandatory disclosure/certification of GAI use.
Verified permitted_with_caution. Standing order (September 2024) permits AI but emphasizes attorney responsibility for verification.
Court-wide standing order June 2024 - Charlotte division. Entry already exists and verified.
No court-wide local rule. Individual judges (Johnston, Fuentes, Coleman) have standing orders.
